title: Oxalis cipoensis
taxonomy: Oxalis cipoensis is classified as a species within the Oxalis genus and the Oxalidaceae family. This species belongs to the order Oxalidales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it within the subclass Rosidae and the division Magnoliophyta. The kingdom to which Oxalis cipoensis belongs is Plantae. It is a flowering plant species native to Brazil.
uses: Oxalis cipoensis is a rare species of wood sorrel native to Brazil, however, information about its medicinal uses is limited. Oxalis cipoensis contains oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
